coverage_window.description: 昨日から現在までの公開市場ドライバー調査

executive_driver_summary.market_sentiment: Neutral to risk-off; strong jobs are being priced as tighter financial conditions rather than simple growth optimism.
executive_driver_summary.primary_driver: US payroll upside surprise causing hawkish Fed repricing, higher US yields and a stronger dollar.
secondary_drivers: CPI, Treasury auctions and FOMC event risk
executive_driver_summary.what_changed_from_yesterday: The market shifted from jobs-data wait-and-see to rates/Fed repricing after May NFP rose 172k and wages rose 3.4% y/y.
executive_driver_summary.largest_trap: Assuming strong employment is automatically bullish for equities; the market is treating it as a higher-for-longer or rate-hike risk.
executive_driver_summary.hrsb_relevance: Rates, SPX, VIX, dollar and credit channels can affect hedge need; final HRSB action requires private portfolio data downstream.

secondary_market_subjects: Oil and Middle East inflation risk
market_lens.market_view: The market is focused less on the jobs headline itself and more on whether it removes the Fed easing path and reintroduces hike risk.
market_lens.change_from_previous_session: Yesterday was event-waiting; today became a rates and dollar shock after the employment report.

five_watch_points.meaning: Psychological and short-dated hedging level; approximate
FX intervention-watch and volatility reawakening risk

causal_chain: BLS reports May NFP +172k with unemployment at 4.3%
Fed cut expectations fade and hike-risk pricing rises
Nasdaq, semiconductors and gold face pressure
SPX/VIX/rates/credit channels become the HRSB monitoring focus
confirmed_facts: BLS reported May nonfarm payrolls +172,000 and unemployment unchanged at 4.3%.
BLS reported average hourly earnings +0.3% m/m and +3.4% y/y.
Reuters/Axios reported US 2Y near 4.15%-4.16% and 10Y near 4.54% after the jobs report.
Axios reported CME FedWatch odds of at least one rate hike by year-end rose to 67% from 45% the prior week.
Reuters reported the dollar firmed and USD/JPY tested around or above 160.
Reuters reported gold fell more than 2% after the strong payrolls report.
interpretations: The dominant transmission channel is rates/Fed repricing, not a generic news selloff.
AI and semiconductor weakness is likely a duration/crowding response to higher yields.
USD/JPY around 160 should be treated as intervention-watch and carry-unwind risk, not proof of intervention.
Low or moderate VIX does not eliminate event-gap risk before CPI and FOMC.

risk_scenarios.condition: US 10Y falls below 4.50% and Nasdaq recovers the post-NFP loss
risk_scenarios.expected_reaction: SPX rebound, VIX softening, gold stabilizes

risk_scenarios.condition: US 10Y stays 4.50%-4.60% and SPX holds near 7500
risk_scenarios.expected_reaction: CPI/FOMC waiting range with short-dated volatility

risk_scenarios.condition: US 2Y exceeds 4.20%, US 10Y exceeds 4.60%, USD/JPY stays above 160 and semis keep falling
risk_scenarios.expected_reaction: Nasdaq-led selloff, higher VIX, credit check required

hiro_attention_points: Watch US 2Y, US 10Y, dollar, VIX and Nasdaq together.
Do not treat strong jobs as automatically bullish for equities.
Prepare for CPI on 2026-06-10 and FOMC on 2026-06-16/17 as the next repricing gates.

fx.update: Dollar rose after strong US jobs data.
Reported lower near 1.1575 after US jobs data.

us: S&P 500 and Nasdaq fell after the strong payrolls report; Nasdaq/semiconductors were more sensitive to higher yields.
europe: European risk tone was cautious with US yields, Middle East risk and tech leadership in focus.
japan: Japan risk is tied to AI/semiconductor momentum and USD/JPY intervention-watch around 160.

rates_and_bonds: US 2Y rose to about 4.15%-4.16% after the jobs report.
US 10Y rose to about 4.54% after the jobs report.
TreasuryDirect schedule indicates 10Y and 30Y securities are auctioned during the second week in relevant months; auction results are not yet available.
commodities: Gold fell more than 2% as strong payrolls boosted higher-rate expectations.
Oil remains mixed but geopolitically sensitive due to Middle East conflict, US-Iran talks, OPEC+ supply expectations and Hormuz constraints.
crypto: Crypto was not identified as a primary HRSB driver due to insufficient reliable current-source confirmation.
central_bank_policy_expectations: Axios reported CME FedWatch odds of at least one year-end rate hike rose to 67% from 45% last week.
Federal Reserve calendar shows the next FOMC meeting on 2026-06-16/17.
critical_information: BLS CPI for May 2026 is scheduled for 2026-06-10 at 08:30 ET.
The jobs report makes CPI the next major confirmation or reversal point for rates and equities.
source_quality_notes: Employment and event dates use primary sources: BLS, Federal Reserve, TreasuryDirect.
Market levels are source-reported and may be stale intraday.
Credit spreads, VVIX, spot dealer gamma and CTA thresholds are data gaps.

headline_vs_actual_driver.actual_market_driver: Hawkish Fed repricing and higher US yields
headline_vs_actual_driver.evidence: BLS +172k NFP; Reuters/Axios reported higher 2Y/10Y yields; Axios reported higher year-end hike odds.
Reuters reported S&P/Nasdaq weakness and chip-stock pressure after strong payrolls.

headline_vs_actual_driver.headline_explanation: Dollar/yen move is only dollar strength
headline_vs_actual_driver.actual_market_driver: Rate-differential plus intervention-watch and carry-unwind market-structure risk
headline_vs_actual_driver.evidence: Reuters reported USD/JPY around/above 160 and official-warning context.

cross_asset_confirmation.notes: Not enough evidence to call it credit-stress driven.
2Y is the cleanest Fed repricing channel.
Gold confirms dollar/yield pressure; oil remains inflation-tail risk.
Need HYG/LQD/CDX/OAS checks before calling credit stress.

flow_risk_map.treasury_auction_bond_supply: Medium: second-week Treasury auctions can test demand after the yield jump; auction statistics not yet available.
flow_risk_map.options_gamma_0dte_opex: Medium-low: SPX/XSP weeklies expire each business day, so short-dated hedging can amplify moves near psychological levels; no direct dealer-flow proof.
flow_risk_map.macro_data_fed_repricing: High: payroll upside moved yields and hike odds; CPI is the next confirmation/reversal event.
flow_risk_map.fx_intervention_carry_unwind: Medium-high: USD/JPY around 160 creates intervention-watch and carry-unwind risk; no actual intervention claimed.
flow_risk_map.commodity_shock: Medium: Middle East, Hormuz and OPEC+ headlines can reprice oil and inflation expectations.
flow_risk_map.earnings_ai_crowding: Medium: AI/semiconductor leadership is sensitive to higher yields and crowding risk.
flow_risk_map.month_end_index_rebalance: Low now; revisit for June OPEX and quarter-end.

trap_risks: Strong jobs may be bearish when it raises the expected policy-rate path.
Low or moderate VIX does not mean event-gap risk is gone before CPI/FOMC.
Oil pullbacks do not erase Middle East inflation-tail risk.
USD/JPY 160 can reverse quickly on intervention headlines or carry unwind.

watch_levels.basis: Reuters source-reported near 4.54%; 4.60% is approximate psychological resistance.

watch_levels.meaning: Psychological level and possible short-dated hedge-pressure area
watch_levels.basis: Approximate psychological threshold; downstream HRSB technical data needed for precision.
Reuters source-reported around/above 160 and official-warning context.

next_24_72h_scenarios.trigger: US 10Y holds 4.50%-4.60% and SPX/Nasdaq digest the NFP selloff
next_24_72h_scenarios.market_reaction: Range trade into CPI with elevated rates sensitivity and moderate volatility risk.
next_24_72h_scenarios.hrsb_relevance: Feed primary_driver, risk_mode, US2Y/US10Y, SPX and VIX data gaps into downstream HRSB.

next_24_72h_scenarios.trigger: US 10Y falls below 4.50%, dollar stalls and Nasdaq recovers post-NFP losses
next_24_72h_scenarios.market_reaction: Risk relief, lower VIX and stabilization in gold/AI leaders.
next_24_72h_scenarios.hrsb_relevance: Treat as rates-shock digestion, not final action advice.

next_24_72h_scenarios.trigger: US 2Y above 4.20%, US 10Y above 4.60%, USD/JPY above 160 and semis keep falling
next_24_72h_scenarios.market_reaction: Nasdaq-led risk-off, higher VIX and need to verify credit stress.
next_24_72h_scenarios.hrsb_relevance: Escalate rates-credit-volatility links to downstream HRSB; no portfolio action decision here.

vix_portfolio_impact_channels: Higher yields can pressure SPX/Nasdaq and increase demand for equity hedges.
CPI/FOMC event gaps can steepen or invert parts of the volatility curve.
USD/JPY intervention-watch risk can trigger cross-asset volatility.
spx_vix_rates_credit_links: US2Y/US10Y up -> growth-duration pressure -> SPX/Nasdaq down -> VIX up risk.
Oil/inflation up -> Fed repricing -> yields up -> credit-spread check required.
If credit spreads remain stable, selloff is more likely rates/valuation than credit stress.
what_to_feed_into_final_hrsb: primary_driver: rates-driven Fed repricing after strong NFP
watch US2Y 4.15%-4.20%, US10Y 4.54%-4.60%, SPX around 7500, USD/JPY 160
next events: CPI 2026-06-10, FOMC 2026-06-16/17, Treasury auctions
data gaps: spot VIX/VVIX, dealer gamma, CTA thresholds, credit spreads

data_sources.name: Reuters via MarketScreener/Yahoo Finance on jobs, yields and equities
Reuters via Yahoo Finance on dollar and yen

data_gaps: Spot VIX and VVIX not directly verified at generation time.
Dealer gamma, 0DTE live option flow and CTA/systematic thresholds not verified.
CDX/HY OAS/IG OAS and real-time credit-spread data not verified.
Copper, Bitcoin and exact Europe/Japan index levels not verified with strong current sources.
Treasury auction results such as tail, bid-to-cover, indirect/direct/dealer takedown are not yet available for future auctions.

high_confidence_points: BLS employment facts and wage data
Rates/Fed repricing as the primary cross-asset driver
medium_confidence_points: AI/semiconductor weakness as a duration/crowding transmission channel
low_confidence_points: Exact real-time spot VIX/VVIX and dealer gamma
Actual dealer, CTA or large-player trading activity
Credit stress without current CDX/OAS confirmation
